Off Calendar Oct-06-2022 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Nov-24-2021 Set For Trial On Nov-14-22 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Documents And Testimony In Support Of Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jul-22-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. CHRIS BRANDES, Esq. of Brock & Gonzales LLP (Tel: 310-294-9494) for plaintiff Sohib Elmahaub;
Atty. APRIL SZABO, Esq. of Atkinson, Andelson, Loya, Ruud & Romo (Tel: 916-923-1200) for defendant Dignity Health;
Atty. KATIE A. COLLINS, Esq. of Weintraub Tobin Chediak Coleman Grodin (Tel: 916-558-6000) for defendant Jones Lang LaSalle Americas, Inc.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT DIGNITY HEALTH's Motion To Seal Documents And Testimony In Support Of Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The motion is denied without prejudice. Any new motion on the same subject must be presented to Judge Karnow.

The motion seeks sealing of an entire agreement in contemplation of an underlying summary judgment motion, relying on the agreement.

A quick review of the agreement shows that much of it is not subject to any protection, e.g., recitals, and boilerplate. E.g., Secs. 1.2 et seq. Thus the motion is overbroad.

It is doubtful corporations have a right to privacy. Weil & Brown, et al., California Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial 8:297 (Rutter: 2022). If they do, a detailed analysis is required. Id. They do enjoy trade secret protections. The Kreisel declaration, which is the only evidence of moving party's entitlement to sealing, does not specify any part of the agreement but treats it a whole. But a statement that the agreement "contains" material which may be a trade secret implies it also "contains" material which is not a trade secret, which in turn implies I must not seal the entire agreement.

If moving party renews the motion, it should first determine which specific parts of the agreement are required to support the underlying summary judgment motion; it is unlikely all parts of the agreement are needed for that purpose.

[Nothing except what is necessary to the determination of the underlying motion should be presented to the court. See Overstock.Com, Inc. v. Goldman Sachs Group, Inc. (2014) 231 CA4th 471, 510, discussed at Weil & Brown, et al., California Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial 9:418.2 (Rutter: 2022).]

If those specific portions do not contain e.g. a trade secret, then the party simply files the materials in the public file, without more. Parts may be redacted. Nothing is filed under seal.

If those portions do require sealing, the party may make a new sealing motion with the usual papers, but it should be accompanied by a "delta document" which is served on the opposing party and delivered to the Judge, but not filed. The court destroys the delta document after the sealing motion is decided. The delta document is a copy of the necessary portions of the agreement (only, not the entire agreement), with the minimum redlines necessary, showing in context which specific words the moving party desires to be sealed. The accompanying admissible evidence should for each part of the document which is redlined, explain why the redline (i.e. sealing) is needed.

Order signed.

Judge: Curtis E.A. Karnow; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/CK)
